RPS10 SOD Experiments

2024-05-21

knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
knitr::opts_knit$set(root.dir = "~/SOD_DADS_microbiome")
library(phyloseq); packageVersion("phyloseq")
## [1] '1.38.0'
library(ggplot2); packageVersion("ggplot2")
## [1] '3.5.1'
library(readr); packageVersion("readr")
## [1] '2.1.5'
library(purrr); packageVersion("purrr")
## [1] '1.0.2'
library(furrr); packageVersion("furrr")
## Loading required package: future
## [1] '0.3.1'
library(dplyr); packageVersion("dplyr")
## 
## Attaching package: 'dplyr'

## 
##     filter, lag
## The following objects are masked from 'package:base':
## 
##     intersect, setdiff, setequal, union
## [1] '1.1.4'

## [1] '1.5.1'
library(metacoder); packageVersion("metacoder")
## This is metacoder version 0.3.7 (stable)

library(vegan);packageVersion("vegan")
## Loading required package: permute

## This is vegan 2.6-6.1

library(agricolae);packageVersion("agricolae")
## [1] '1.3.7'
library(magrittr);packageVersion("magrittr")
## 
## Attaching package: 'magrittr'

##

## [1] '2.0.3'
library(data.table);packageVersion("data.table")
## [1] '1.15.4'
library(ggbeeswarm);packageVersion("ggbeeswarm")
## [1] '0.7.2'
library(DESeq2);packageVersion("DESeq2")
## Loading required package: GenomicRanges
## Loading required package: SummarizedExperiment

##     rowOrderStats, rowProds, rowQuantiles, rowRanges, rowRanks,

## [1] '1.34.0'
library(cowplot);packageVersion("cowplot")
## [1] '1.1.3'
theme_set(theme_cowplot())
library(doParallel);packageVersion("doParallel")
## Loading required package: foreach
## 
## Attaching package: 'foreach'
## The following objects are masked from 'package:purrr':
## 
##     accumulate, when
## Loading required package: iterators
## Loading required package: parallel
## [1] '1.0.17'
library(viridis);packageVersion("viridis")
## Loading required package: viridisLite
## [1] '0.6.5'
library(geosphere)

Load data

load("Data/RPS10/filtered_data/sod_pruned.RData")
load("Data/RPS10/filtered_data/sod_prunereads_5asv.RData")

filtered_read_counts<-read.csv("Data/RPS10/filtered_data/RPS10_subset_track_reads_sod.csv")
summary(filtered_read_counts)
##       X                 input           filtered        denoisedF     
##  Length:98          Min.   :  2718   Min.   :  2718   Min.   :     6  
##  Class :character   1st Qu.: 21895   1st Qu.: 21880   1st Qu.:  6704  
##  Mode  :character   Median : 42818   Median : 42780   Median : 15625  
##                     Mean   : 49591   Mean   : 49579   Mean   : 22324  
##                     3rd Qu.: 67962   3rd Qu.: 67938   3rd Qu.: 28861  
##                     Max.   :280147   Max.   :280126   Max.   :185346  
##    denoisedR          merged          nonchim      
##  Min.   :     2   Min.   :     2   Min.   :     2  
##  1st Qu.:  6692   1st Qu.:  6486   1st Qu.:  6486  
##  Median : 15586   Median : 14583   Median : 14582  
##  Mean   : 22313   Mean   : 20821   Mean   : 20646  
##  3rd Qu.: 28856   3rd Qu.: 25856   3rd Qu.: 25367  
##  Max.   :185244   Max.   :175106   Max.   :175106
new_names <- sapply(strsplit(sod_prunereads_5asv.ps@sam_data$Sample_nameRps10, "-"), function(x) {
  substr(x[1], 3, nchar(x[1]))
})

sod_prunereads_5asv.ps@sam_data$Sample_nameRps10 <- new_names

Bar plot agglomerated by species-using filtered dataset and facet wrapped by treatment

RPS10data <- sod_prunereads_5asv.ps %>%
  transform_sample_counts(function(x) {x / sum(x)}) %>% 
  tax_glom(taxrank = "Order") %>% 
  psmelt() %>% 
  #mutate(across(Kingdom:Species, ~ ifelse(is.na(.), "Unclassified", .))) %>%  
  arrange(desc(Abundance))

abund_plot <- ggplot(RPS10data, aes(x = Sample_nameRps10, y = Abundance, fill = Order)) + 
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", position = "stack", color = "black", size = 0.2) +
  scale_fill_viridis_d() +
  scale_color_viridis_d() +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(
    y = "Relative Abundance",
    fill = "Order"
  ) +
  facet_grid(~factor(Treatuntreat_EP), scales = "free_x") +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 90, hjust = 1, vjust = 0.5, size = 16),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 20),  # Increase y-axis label size
    axis.title.x = element_blank(),  # Remove the x-axis title if needed
    axis.title.y = element_text(size = 20), 
    panel.grid.major = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    legend.position = "bottom",
    legend.text = element_text(size = 20),
    legend.title = element_text(size = 20, face = "bold"),
    legend.key.size = unit(1, "lines"),
    strip.text = element_text(size = 24, face= "bold"),
    strip.background = element_blank()
  ) +
  guides(
    fill = guide_legend(
      reverse = FALSE,
      keywidth = 1,
      keyheight = 1,
      title.position = "top",
      title.hjust = 0.5,
      label.theme = element_text(size = 20)
    )
  )
## Warning: Using `size` aesthetic for lines was deprecated in ggplot2 3.4.0.
## ℹ Please use `linewidth` instead.
## This warning is displayed once every 8 hours.
## Call `lifecycle::last_lifecycle_warnings()` to see where this warning was
## generated.
abund_plot <- abund_plot + 
  scale_x_discrete(labels = function(x) gsub("Sample_nameRps10", "", x))  # Remove unwanted text

abund_plot

# Save the plot
ggsave("Figures/SOD/RPS10/relabundance_plot_treatment_relabund_order_sod.pdf", abund_plot, width = 24, height = 10)

Make a similar plot, but color by Family instead

RPS10data <- sod_prunereads_5asv.ps %>%
  transform_sample_counts(function(x) {x / sum(x)}) %>% 
  tax_glom(taxrank = "Family") %>% 
  psmelt() %>% 
  #mutate(across(Kingdom:Species, ~ ifelse(is.na(.), "Unclassified", .))) %>%  
  arrange(desc(Abundance))

abund_plot <- ggplot(RPS10data, aes(x = Sample_nameRps10, y = Abundance, fill = Family)) + 
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", position = "stack", color = "black", size = 0.2) +
  scale_fill_viridis_d() +
  scale_color_viridis_d() +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(
    y = "Relative Abundance",
    fill = "Family"
  ) +
  facet_grid(~factor(Treatuntreat_EP), scales = "free_x") +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 90, hjust = 1, vjust = 0.5, size = 16),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 20),  # Increase y-axis label size
    axis.title.x = element_blank(),  # Remove the x-axis title if needed
    axis.title.y = element_text(size = 20), 
    panel.grid.major = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    legend.position = "bottom",
    legend.text = element_text(size = 20),
    legend.title = element_text(size = 20, face = "bold"),
    legend.key.size = unit(1, "lines"),
    strip.text = element_text(size = 24, face= "bold"),
    strip.background = element_blank()
  ) +
  guides(
    fill = guide_legend(
      reverse = FALSE,
      keywidth = 1,
      keyheight = 1,
      title.position = "top",
      title.hjust = 0.5,
      label.theme = element_text(size = 20)
    )
  )

abund_plot <- abund_plot + 
  scale_x_discrete(labels = function(x) gsub("Sample_nameRps10", "", x))  # Remove unwanted text

abund_plot

# Save the plot
ggsave("Figures/SOD/RPS10/relabundance_plot_treatment_relabund_family_sod.pdf", abund_plot, width = 24, height = 10)

Bar plot agglomerated by order-facetted by fire factor

RPS10data <- sod_prunereads_5asv.ps %>%
  transform_sample_counts(function(x) {x / sum(x)}) %>% 
  tax_glom(taxrank = "Order") %>% 
  psmelt() %>% 
  #mutate(across(Kingdom:Species, ~ ifelse(is.na(.), "Unclassified", .))) %>%  
  arrange(desc(Abundance))

abund_plot <- ggplot(RPS10data, aes(x = Sample_nameRps10, y = Abundance, fill = Order)) + 
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", position = "stack", color = "black", size = 0.2) +
  scale_fill_viridis_d() +
  scale_color_viridis_d() +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(
    y = "Relative Abundance",
    fill = "Order"
  ) +
  facet_grid(~factor(Fire_Mod), scales = "free_x") +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 90, hjust = 1, vjust = 0.5, size = 16),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 20),  # Increase y-axis label size
    axis.title.x = element_blank(),  # Remove the x-axis title if needed
    axis.title.y = element_text(size = 20), 
    panel.grid.major = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    legend.position = "bottom",
    legend.text = element_text(size = 20),
    legend.title = element_text(size = 20, face = "bold"),
    legend.key.size = unit(1, "lines"),
    strip.text = element_text(size = 24, face= "bold"),
    strip.background = element_blank()
  ) +
  guides(
    fill = guide_legend(
      reverse = FALSE,
      keywidth = 1,
      keyheight = 1,
      title.position = "top",
      title.hjust = 0.5,
      label.theme = element_text(size = 20)
    )
  )

abund_plot <- abund_plot + 
  scale_x_discrete(labels = function(x) gsub("Sample_nameRps10", "", x))  # Remove unwanted text

abund_plot

# Save the plot
ggsave("Figures/SOD/RPS10/relabundance_plot_fire_relabund_order_sod.pdf", abund_plot, width = 24, height = 10)

Bar plot agglomerated by order-using filtered dataset and faceted by yr since trt

RPS10data <- sod_prunereads_5asv.ps %>%
  transform_sample_counts(function(x) {x / sum(x)}) %>% 
  tax_glom(taxrank = "Order") %>% 
  psmelt() %>% 
  #mutate(across(Kingdom:Species, ~ ifelse(is.na(.), "Unclassified", .))) %>%  
  arrange(desc(Abundance))

abund_plot <- ggplot(RPS10data, aes(x = Sample_nameRps10, y = Abundance, fill = Order)) + 
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", position = "stack", color = "black", size = 0.2) +
  scale_fill_viridis_d() +
  scale_color_viridis_d() +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(
    y = "Relative Abundance",
    fill = "Family"
  ) +
  facet_grid(~factor(YrSinceTrt_BR), scales = "free_x") +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 90, hjust = 1, vjust = 0.5, size = 16),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 20),  # Increase y-axis label size
    axis.title.x = element_blank(),  # Remove the x-axis title if needed
    axis.title.y = element_text(size = 20), 
    panel.grid.major = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    legend.position = "bottom",
    legend.text = element_text(size = 20),
    legend.title = element_text(size = 20, face = "bold"),
    legend.key.size = unit(1, "lines"),
    strip.text = element_text(size = 24, face= "bold"),
    strip.background = element_blank()
  ) +
  guides(
    fill = guide_legend(
      reverse = FALSE,
      keywidth = 1,
      keyheight = 1,
      title.position = "top",
      title.hjust = 0.5,
      label.theme = element_text(size = 20)
    )
  )

abund_plot <- abund_plot + 
  scale_x_discrete(labels = function(x) gsub("Sample_nameRps10", "", x))  # Remove unwanted text

abund_plot

# Save the plot
ggsave("Figures/SOD/RPS10/relabundance_plot_yrsincetrt_relabund_order_sod.pdf", abund_plot, width = 24, height = 10)

Let’s look top taxa at order level

top40 <- names(sort(taxa_sums(sod_prunereads_5asv.ps), decreasing=TRUE))[1:40]
ps.top40 <- transform_sample_counts(sod_prunereads_5asv.ps, function(OTU) OTU/sum(OTU))
#ps.top40@tax_table[is.na(ps.top40@tax_table)] <- "Unclassified"
ps.top40 <- tax_glom(ps.top40, taxrank = "Order")
ps.top40 <- prune_taxa(top40, ps.top40)

pdf("Figures/SOD/RPS10/top40_genus_sod.pdf", width = 34, height = 20)
plot_bar(ps.top40, x="Sample_nameRps10", fill="Order") + facet_wrap(~Treatuntreat_EP, scales="free_x")
dev.off()
## png 
##   2

Let’s look top taxa at family level

top40 <- names(sort(taxa_sums(sod_prunereads_5asv.ps), decreasing=TRUE))[1:40]
ps.top40 <- transform_sample_counts(sod_prunereads_5asv.ps, function(OTU) OTU/sum(OTU))
#ps.top40@tax_table[is.na(ps.top40@tax_table)] <- "Unclassified"
ps.top40 <- tax_glom(ps.top40, taxrank = "Family")
ps.top40 <- prune_taxa(top40, ps.top40)

pdf("Figures/SOD/RPS10/top40_family_sod.pdf", width = 34, height = 20)
plot_bar(ps.top40, x="Sample_nameRps10", fill="Family") + facet_wrap(~Treatuntreat_EP, scales="free_x")
dev.off()
## png 
##   2

Let’s look top taxa at genus level

top40 <- names(sort(taxa_sums(sod_prunereads_5asv.ps), decreasing=TRUE))[1:40]
ps.top40 <- transform_sample_counts(sod_prunereads_5asv.ps, function(OTU) OTU/sum(OTU))
#ps.top40@tax_table[is.na(ps.top40@tax_table)] <- "Unclassified"
ps.top40 <- tax_glom(ps.top40, taxrank = "Genus")
ps.top40 <- prune_taxa(top40, ps.top40)

pdf("Figures/SOD/RPS10/top40_genus_sod.pdf", width = 34, height = 20)
plot_bar(ps.top40, x="Sample_nameRps10", fill="Genus") + facet_wrap(~Treatuntreat_EP, scales="free_x")
dev.off()
## png 
##   2
data <-
  sod_prunereads_5asv.ps %>%
  tax_glom("Species") %>%
  psmelt() %>%
  as_tibble()

data_summed <- data %>%
  filter(Abundance > 0) %>%
  group_by(Kingdom, Phylum, Class, Order, Family, Genus, Species) %>%
  summarise(Abundance = sum(Abundance),
            Prevalence = n_distinct(Sample)) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  mutate(Total_Abundance = sum(Abundance)) %>%
  mutate(Percentage = 100*(Abundance / Total_Abundance))
## `summarise()` has grouped output by 'Kingdom', 'Phylum', 'Class', 'Order',
## 'Family', 'Genus'. You can override using the `.groups` argument.
write.csv(data_summed, file="Results/SOD/RPS10/mostabundanttaxa_abundance_sod_species.csv",row.names = TRUE)

data <-
  sod_prunereads_5asv.ps %>%
  tax_glom("Genus") %>%
  psmelt() %>%
  as_tibble()

data_summed <- data %>%
  filter(Abundance > 0) %>%
  group_by(Kingdom, Phylum, Class, Order, Family, Genus) %>%
  summarise(Abundance = sum(Abundance),
            Prevalence = n_distinct(Sample)) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  mutate(Total_Abundance = sum(Abundance)) %>%
  mutate(Percentage = 100*(Abundance / Total_Abundance))
## `summarise()` has grouped output by 'Kingdom', 'Phylum', 'Class', 'Order',
## 'Family'. You can override using the `.groups` argument.
write.csv(data_summed, file="Results/SOD/RPS10/mostabundanttaxa_abundance_sod_genus.csv",row.names = TRUE)

data <-
  sod_prunereads_5asv.ps %>%
  tax_glom("Family") %>%
  psmelt() %>%
  as_tibble()

data_summed <- data %>%
  filter(Abundance > 0) %>%
  group_by(Kingdom, Phylum, Class, Order, Family) %>%
  summarise(Abundance = sum(Abundance),
            Prevalence = n_distinct(Sample)) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  mutate(Total_Abundance = sum(Abundance)) %>%
  mutate(Percentage = 100*(Abundance / Total_Abundance))
## `summarise()` has grouped output by 'Kingdom', 'Phylum', 'Class', 'Order'. You
## can override using the `.groups` argument.
write.csv(data_summed, file="Results/SOD/RPS10/mostabundanttaxa_abundance_sod_family.csv",row.names = TRUE)

data1 <-
  sod_prunereads_5asv.ps %>%
  tax_glom("Species") %>%
  transform_sample_counts(function(x) {x/sum(x)} ) %>%
  psmelt() %>%
  as_tibble()

data_summed1 <- data1 %>%
  filter(Abundance > 0) %>%
  group_by(Treatuntreat_EP, Kingdom, Phylum, Class, Order, Family, Genus, Species) %>%
  summarise(Abundance = sum(Abundance),
            Prevalence = n_distinct(Sample)) 
## `summarise()` has grouped output by 'Treatuntreat_EP', 'Kingdom', 'Phylum',
## 'Class', 'Order', 'Family', 'Genus'. You can override using the `.groups`
## argument.
write.csv(data_summed1, file="Results/SOD/RPS10/mostabundanttaxa_relativeabundance_untreatedtreated_sod.csv",row.names = TRUE)

data2 <-
  sod_prunereads_5asv.ps %>%
  tax_glom("Species") %>%
  psmelt() %>%
  as_tibble()

# Calculate percentages
data_summed2 <- data2 %>%
  filter(Abundance > 0) %>%
  group_by(Treatuntreat_EP, Kingdom, Phylum, Class, Order, Family, Genus, Species) %>%
  summarise(Abundance = sum(Abundance),
            Prevalence = n_distinct(Sample))
## `summarise()` has grouped output by 'Treatuntreat_EP', 'Kingdom', 'Phylum',
## 'Class', 'Order', 'Family', 'Genus'. You can override using the `.groups`
## argument.
write.csv(data_summed2, file="Results/SOD/RPS10/mostabundanttaxa_untreatedtreated_sod.csv",row.names = TRUE)

Let’s look at spacial placement of different plots

pdf("Figures/SOD/RPS10/location_of_plots.pdf", width = 20, height = 12)
map_plot <- ggplot(sod_prunereads_5asv.ps@sam_data, aes(x = Longitude, y = Latitude)) +
  geom_text(aes(color = Fire_Mod, label = Sample_nameRps10), size = 2.5, 
            check_overlap = TRUE) +
  labs(x = "Longitude", y = "Latitude", title = "Sampling Locations") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(size = 20), 
    axis.title = element_text(size = 16),
    axis.text = element_text(color = "black"),
    legend.title = element_text(size = 14),
    legend.text = element_text(size = 12)
  )
print(map_plot)
dev.off()
## png 
##   2

Let’s normalize our data in preparation for beta diversity analyses

#Prune ASVs not present in least 5 times (across one more more samples)
#sod_prunereads_5asv.ps<- phyloseq::prune_taxa(taxa_sums(sod_prunereads.ps) > 5, sod_prunereads.ps)
sdt = data.table::data.table(as(sample_data(sod_prunereads_5asv.ps), "data.frame"),
                 TotalReads = sample_sums(sod_prunereads_5asv.ps), keep.rownames = TRUE)
min_reads<-min(sdt$TotalReads)

rarefied.ps <- rarefy_even_depth(sod_prunereads_5asv.ps, sample.size = min_reads, rngseed = 100)
## `set.seed(100)` was used to initialize repeatable random subsampling.
## Please record this for your records so others can reproduce.
## Try `set.seed(100); .Random.seed` for the full vector
## ...
## 26OTUs were removed because they are no longer 
## present in any sample after random subsampling
## ...
#Relative abundance
strat_rel.ps<-transform_sample_counts(sod_prunereads_5asv.ps, function(x) x / sum(x))

Alpha diversity-use non-normalized or pruned data

Let’s start with t-test as point of comparison with anova analysis

set.seed(1)
AD <- estimate_richness(sod_prunereads.ps, measures = c("Observed", "InvSimpson", "Shannon"))
rownames(AD) <- gsub("\\.", "-", rownames(AD))

# Combine richness estimates with sample metadata
AD <- cbind(AD, sample_data(sod_prunereads.ps)[row.names(AD), c("Treatuntreat_EP", "Lineage_EP", "SoilBin", "VegBin")])

# Convert to data.table and keep row names as a column
AD <- data.table(AD, keep.rownames = TRUE)

# Reshape the data frame
AD <- melt(AD, id.vars = c('rn', c("Treatuntreat_EP", "Lineage_EP", "SoilBin", "VegBin")))

# Now conduct t-tests (assuming you have appropriate factors)
factors <- c("Treatuntreat_EP", "Lineage_EP", "SoilBin", "VegBin")
# Iterate through each factor
ttest_list <- list()

# Iterate through each factor
for (factor_name in factors) {
  # Initialize an empty data.table to store t-test results for each alpha diversity measure
  ttest <- data.table(alpha_index = character(), pvalue = numeric())
  
  # Iterate through each alpha diversity measure
  for (alphad in c("InvSimpson", "Shannon", "Observed")) {
    # Perform t-test
    ttest_res <- t.test(value ~ get(factor_name), data = AD[variable == alphad], var.equal = TRUE)
    
    # Extract p-value from t-test results and store it in the data.table
    p_value <- ttest_res$p.value
    ttest <- rbind(ttest, data.table(alpha_index = alphad, pvalue = p_value))
  }
  
  # Add t-test results for the current factor to the list
  ttest_list[[factor_name]] <- ttest
}

# Print t-test results
for (factor_name in factors) {
  cat(paste("T-Test Results for Factor:", factor_name), "\n")
  print(knitr::kable(ttest_list[[factor_name]], format = "markdown"))
  cat("\n")
}
## T-Test Results for Factor: Treatuntreat_EP 
## 
## 
## |alpha_index |    pvalue|
## |:-----------|---------:|
## |InvSimpson  | 0.9941577|
## |Shannon     | 0.9203749|
## |Observed    | 0.9596284|
## 
## T-Test Results for Factor: Lineage_EP 
## 
## 
## |alpha_index |    pvalue|
## |:-----------|---------:|
## |InvSimpson  | 0.0195298|
## |Shannon     | 0.0155486|
## |Observed    | 0.2157685|
## 
## T-Test Results for Factor: SoilBin 
## 
## 
## |alpha_index |    pvalue|
## |:-----------|---------:|
## |InvSimpson  | 0.4802264|
## |Shannon     | 0.4035668|
## |Observed    | 0.7961629|
## 
## T-Test Results for Factor: VegBin 
## 
## 
## |alpha_index |    pvalue|
## |:-----------|---------:|
## |InvSimpson  | 0.0523886|
## |Shannon     | 0.0642351|
## |Observed    | 0.4607900|

Let’s now proceed with ANOVA analyses and then compile our alpha diversity stats

# Initialize an empty data.table to store combined ANOVA results for all factors
set.seed(1)
anova_list <- list()

AD <- estimate_richness(sod_prunereads.ps, measures = c("Observed", "InvSimpson", "Shannon"))
rownames(AD) <- gsub("\\.", "-", rownames(AD))

# Combine richness estimates with sample metadata
AD <- cbind(AD, sample_data(sod_prunereads.ps)[row.names(AD), c("Treatuntreat_EP", "Fire_Mod", "YrSinceTrt_BR")])

# Convert to data.table and keep row names as a column
AD <- data.table(AD, keep.rownames = TRUE)

# Reshape the data frame
AD <- melt(AD, id.vars = c('rn', c("Treatuntreat_EP", "Fire_Mod", "YrSinceTrt_BR")))

factors2 <- c("Treatuntreat_EP", "Fire_Mod", "YrSinceTrt_BR")

# Initialize a vector to store factor names for which a plot has been created
plotted_factors <- character()
 

final_anova_table <- data.table(factor_name = character(), alpha_index = character(), pvalue = numeric(), df_between = numeric(), df_within = numeric(), ss_between = numeric(), ss_within = numeric(), ms_between = numeric(), ms_within = numeric(), F_value = numeric())

for (factor_name in factors2) {
  # Initialize an empty data.table to store ANOVA results for each alpha diversity measure
  anova <- data.table(factor_name = character(), alpha_index = character(), pvalue = numeric(), df_between = numeric(), df_within = numeric(), ss_between = numeric(), ss_within = numeric(), ms_between = numeric(), ms_within = numeric(), F_value = numeric())
  
  # Iterate through each alpha diversity measure
  for (alphad in c("Observed","InvSimpson", "Shannon")) {
    # Perform ANOVA
    anova_res <- aov(value ~ get(factor_name), data = AD[variable == alphad])
    
    # Extract relevant statistics from ANOVA results
    anova_summary <- summary(anova_res)
    
    # Extract p-value from ANOVA results
    p_value <- anova_summary[[1]]$`Pr(>F)`[1]
    
    # Extract other statistics
    df_between <- anova_summary[[1]]$"Df"[1]
    df_within <- anova_summary[[1]]$"Df"[2]
    ss_between <- anova_summary[[1]]$"Sum Sq"[1]
    ss_within <- anova_summary[[1]]$"Sum Sq"[2]
    ms_between <- anova_summary[[1]]$"Mean Sq"[1]
    ms_within <- anova_summary[[1]]$"Mean Sq"[2]
    F_value <- anova_summary[[1]]$"F value"[1]
    
    # Store statistics in the data.table
    anova <- rbind(anova, data.table(factor_name = factor_name, alpha_index = alphad, pvalue = p_value, df_between = df_between, df_within = df_within, ss_between = ss_between, ss_within = ss_within, ms_between = ms_between, ms_within = ms_within, F_value = F_value))
  }
  
  # Append ANOVA results for the current factor to the final table
  final_anova_table <- rbind(final_anova_table, anova)
}

# Print final ANOVA table
print(knitr::kable(final_anova_table, format = "markdown"))
## 
## 
## |factor_name     |alpha_index |    pvalue| df_between| df_within|  ss_between|  ss_within| ms_between|  ms_within|   F_value|
## |:---------------|:-----------|---------:|----------:|---------:|-----------:|----------:|----------:|----------:|---------:|
## |Treatuntreat_EP |Observed    | 0.9596284|          1|        85|   0.0830648| 2739.25027|  0.0830648| 32.2264737| 0.0025775|
## |Treatuntreat_EP |InvSimpson  | 0.9941577|          1|        85|   0.0001495|  235.54838|  0.0001495|  2.7711574| 0.0000539|
## |Treatuntreat_EP |Shannon     | 0.9203749|          1|        85|   0.0033196|   28.07128|  0.0033196|  0.3302504| 0.0100518|
## |Fire_Mod        |Observed    | 0.0612174|          2|        84| 176.2604588| 2563.07287| 88.1302294| 30.5127723| 2.8883062|
## |Fire_Mod        |InvSimpson  | 0.0689816|          2|        84|  14.5287255|  221.01980|  7.2643627|  2.6311881| 2.7608679|
## |Fire_Mod        |Shannon     | 0.0115994|          2|        84|   2.8264974|   25.24811|  1.4132487|  0.3005727| 4.7018533|
## |YrSinceTrt_BR   |Observed    | 0.4544220|          4|        82| 118.0864662| 2621.24687| 29.5216165| 31.9664252| 0.9235195|
## |YrSinceTrt_BR   |InvSimpson  | 0.6038387|          4|        82|   7.6244753|  227.92405|  1.9061188|  2.7795616| 0.6857624|
## |YrSinceTrt_BR   |Shannon     | 0.8616639|          4|        82|   0.4357690|   27.63883|  0.1089422|  0.3370590| 0.3232142|
write.csv(final_anova_table, "Results/SOD/RPS10/final_anova_table_sod.csv", row.names = FALSE)

Let’s make faceted boxplots for our alpha diversity analyses

# Estimate alpha diversity
set.seed(1)
AD <- estimate_richness(sod_prunereads.ps, measures = c("Observed", "InvSimpson", "Shannon"))
rownames(AD) <- gsub("\\.", "-", rownames(AD))

# Combine richness estimates with sample metadata
AD <- cbind(AD, sample_data(sod_prunereads_5asv.ps)[rownames(AD), c("Treatuntreat_EP", "Fire_Mod", "YrSinceTrt_BR")])


variables <- c("Treatuntreat_EP", "Fire_Mod", "YrSinceTrt_BR")


# Create an empty list to store plots
plots <- list()

# Loop through each variable and create the plot
for (variable in variables) {
  p <- plot_richness(sod_prunereads.ps, x = variable, measures = c("Shannon", "InvSimpson", "Observed")) + 
    geom_boxplot()
  plots[[variable]] <- p
}

# Arrange plots in a grid
plots_arrange <- gridExtra::grid.arrange(
  plots[["Treatuntreat_EP"]], plots[["Fire_Mod"]], plots[["YrSinceTrt_BR"]], plots[["YearSam_EP"]], plots[["Lineage_EP"]], plots[["VegBin"]], plots[["SoilBin"]],ncol=1)

# Add annotations to the top of each plot
plots_arrange_with_text <- cowplot::ggdraw() +
  cowplot::draw_plot(plots_arrange) +
  cowplot::draw_plot("Treatuntreat_EP", x = 0.28, y = 0.98) +
  cowplot::draw_plot("Fire_EP", x = 0.28, y = 0.98) +
  cowplot::draw_plot("YrSinceTrt_BR", x = 0.28, y = 0.98) 
## Warning in as_grob.default(plot): Cannot convert object of class character into
## a grob.
## Warning in as_grob.default(plot): Cannot convert object of class character into
## a grob.
## Warning in as_grob.default(plot): Cannot convert object of class character into
## a grob.
# Save the arranged plots
ggsave(plots_arrange_with_text, path = "Figures/SOD/RPS10", filename = "alpha_diversity_each_factor_sod.pdf", 
       width = 16, height = 24)

Let’s perform TUKEY HSD tests

# Define your variables of interest
set.seed(1)
variables <- c("Treatuntreat_EP", "Fire_Mod", "YrSinceTrt_BR")
measures <- c("Observed", "InvSimpson", "Shannon")

# Create an empty list to store ANOVA and Tukey's results
tukey_results <- list()

# Loop through each variable and measure
for (variable in variables) {
  for (measure in measures) {
    # Subset the data for the current variable and measure
    data_subset <- AD[, c(variable, measure)]
    
    # Perform ANOVA
    formula <- as.formula(paste(measure, "~ ."))
    anova_res <- aov(formula, data = data_subset)
    
    # Perform Tukey's post-hoc test
    tukey_result <- HSD.test(anova_res, variable)
    
    # Store Tukey's results
    print(tukey_result)
  }
}
## $statistics

Beta diversity-PERMANOVA analyses-ASV counts transformed (rel. abundance)

#I used the filtered dataset (removed low abundance counts less than 2)
set.seed(1)
mapping <- as.data.frame(sample_data(strat_rel.ps))

mapping2=mapping[, c("Treatuntreat_EP", "Fire_Mod", "YrSinceTrt_BR")]

ASV_tables_bray <- phyloseq::distance(strat_rel.ps, method = "bray")

df_metadata <- data.frame(sample_data(strat_rel.ps))

df_metadata<-df_metadata[, c("Treatuntreat_EP", "Fire_Mod", "YrSinceTrt_BR")]

pvals <- sapply(colnames(mapping2), function(variable){
  
  #Create formula for adonis() 
  form1<-as.formula(paste("ASV_tables_bray", variable, sep="~"))
  
  res <- adonis2(form1, data = df_metadata, permutations = 9999)
  
  # extract pvalue from adonis output
  pval <- res$`Pr(>F)`[[1]] # we take the first p value which corresponds to the variable of interest
  return(pval)
})

#Adjust p_values with fdr correction
pval_adj <- p.adjust(pvals, method = "fdr")

#Find significant variables with lower than 0.05 for their p values
save.pval.adonis0.05_braycurtis <- pval_adj[pval_adj < 0.05]
save.pval.adonis0.05_braycurtis
## Fire_Mod 
##   0.0015
print(pval_adj)
## Treatuntreat_EP        Fire_Mod   YrSinceTrt_BR 
##         0.07460         0.00150         0.05685

Compile PERMANOVA stats

# Perform PERMANOVA -main effects
set.seed(1)
res <- adonis2(ASV_tables_bray ~Treatuntreat_EP+Fire_Mod+YrSinceTrt_BR, data = df_metadata, permutations = 9999)

# Extract data from res
res_data <- data.frame(res)
colnames(res_data)[colnames(res_data) == "Pr..F."] <- "pr_f"

# Remove the last two rows (Residual and Total)
res_data <- res_data[-c(nrow(res_data)-1, nrow(res_data)),]

# Add FDR and Bonferroni adjusted p-values
res_data$FDR_adj_p <- p.adjust(res_data$pr_f, method = "fdr")
res_data$Bonferroni_adj_p <- p.adjust(res_data$pr_f, method = "bonferroni")
res_data$Factor <- rownames(res_data)

# Reorder columns
res_data <- res_data[, c("Factor", "Df", "SumOfSqs", "R2", "F", "pr_f", "FDR_adj_p", "Bonferroni_adj_p")]
rownames(res_data) <- NULL

# Perform beta dispersion analysis for main effects
beta_disp_results <- lapply(c("Treatuntreat_EP", "Fire_Mod", "YrSinceTrt_BR"), function(variable) {
  res_betadisper <- betadisper(d = ASV_tables_bray, group = df_metadata[[variable]])
  perm_test_res <- permutest(res_betadisper)
  pval_betadisper <- perm_test_res$tab$`Pr(>F)`[[1]]
  
  data.frame(Factor = variable, P_Value_BetaDispersion = pval_betadisper)
})

beta_disp_results_df <- do.call(rbind, beta_disp_results)

# Merge PERMANOVA results with beta dispersion results
final_results <- merge(res_data, beta_disp_results_df, by = "Factor", all.x = TRUE)

# Reorder columns
final_results <- final_results[, c("Factor", "Df", "SumOfSqs", "R2", "F", "pr_f", "FDR_adj_p", "Bonferroni_adj_p", "P_Value_BetaDispersion")]

# Print the table
print(final_results, row.names = FALSE)
##           Factor Df  SumOfSqs         R2        F   pr_f FDR_adj_p
##         Fire_Mod  1 0.9787818 0.02416617 2.119705 0.0002   0.00060
##  Treatuntreat_EP  1 0.6328369 0.01562478 1.370507 0.0639   0.09585
##    YrSinceTrt_BR  3 1.4884656 0.03675029 1.074502 0.2638   0.26380
##  Bonferroni_adj_p P_Value_BetaDispersion
##            0.0006                  0.017
##            0.1917                  0.296
##            0.7914                  0.001
# Save results
write.csv(final_results, file = "Results/SOD/RPS10/PERMANOVA_interactions_with_betadisper_maineffects.csv", row.names = FALSE)

Compile PERMANOVA stats-interaction

# Perform PERMANOVA -main effects
set.seed(1)
res <- adonis2(ASV_tables_bray ~ Treatuntreat_EP*YrSinceTrt_BR*Fire_Mod, data = df_metadata, permutations = 9999)
# Extract data from res
res_data <- data.frame(res)
colnames(res_data)[colnames(res_data) == "Pr..F."] <- "pr_f"

# Remove the last two rows (Residual and Total)
res_data <- res_data[-c(nrow(res_data)-1, nrow(res_data)),]

# Add FDR and Bonferroni adjusted p-values
res_data$FDR_adj_p <- p.adjust(res_data$pr_f, method = "fdr")
res_data$Bonferroni_adj_p <- p.adjust(res_data$pr_f, method = "bonferroni")
res_data$Factor <- rownames(res_data)

# Reorder columns
res_data <- res_data[, c("Factor", "Df", "SumOfSqs", "R2", "F", "pr_f", "FDR_adj_p", "Bonferroni_adj_p")]
rownames(res_data) <- NULL

# Perform beta dispersion analysis for main effects
beta_disp_results <- lapply(c("Treatuntreat_EP", "Fire_Mod"), function(variable) {
  res_betadisper <- betadisper(d = ASV_tables_bray, group = df_metadata[[variable]])
  perm_test_res <- permutest(res_betadisper)
  pval_betadisper <- perm_test_res$tab$`Pr(>F)`[[1]]
  
  data.frame(Factor = variable, P_Value_BetaDispersion = pval_betadisper)
})

beta_disp_results_df <- do.call(rbind, beta_disp_results)

# Merge PERMANOVA results with beta dispersion results
final_results <- merge(res_data, beta_disp_results_df, by = "Factor", all.x = TRUE)

# Reorder columns
final_results <- final_results[, c("Factor", "Df", "SumOfSqs", "R2", "F", "pr_f", "FDR_adj_p", "Bonferroni_adj_p", "P_Value_BetaDispersion")]

# Print the table
print(final_results, row.names = FALSE)
##                  Factor Df  SumOfSqs         R2        F   pr_f FDR_adj_p
##                Fire_Mod  1 0.8384632 0.02070170 1.816842 0.0032 0.0128000
##         Treatuntreat_EP  1 0.6328369 0.01562478 1.371276 0.0634 0.1198667
##           YrSinceTrt_BR  3 1.6287842 0.04021477 1.176455 0.0899 0.1198667
##  YrSinceTrt_BR:Fire_Mod  2 0.9439665 0.02330658 1.022727 0.4229 0.4229000
##  Bonferroni_adj_p P_Value_BetaDispersion
##            0.0128                  0.017
##            0.2536                  0.296
##            0.3596                     NA
##            1.0000                     NA
# Save results
write.csv(final_results, file = "Results/SOD/RPS10/PERMANOVA_interactions_with_betadisper_firemod.csv", row.names = FALSE)

Let’s now examine beta diversity through ordination plots

We’ll will examine outputs for rarefied data as well as data adjusted by relative abundance We’ll now make ordination plots on data for which we’ve calculated relative abundance

#Relative abundance
set.seed(1)
ps_NMDS = ordinate(strat_rel.ps, 
                   method="NMDS", 
                   distance="bray")
## Run 0 stress 0.109065 
## Run 1 stress 0.1132285 
## Run 2 stress 0.112343 
## Run 3 stress 0.1122217 
## Run 4 stress 0.1086383 
## ... New best solution
## ... Procrustes: rmse 0.08973638  max resid 0.2663288 
## Run 5 stress 0.1090746 
## ... Procrustes: rmse 0.09218401  max resid 0.315221 
## Run 6 stress 0.1097841 
## Run 7 stress 0.1077114 
## ... New best solution
## ... Procrustes: rmse 0.05515573  max resid 0.2686255 
## Run 8 stress 0.1110021 
## Run 9 stress 0.1124331 
## Run 10 stress 0.1085894 
## Run 11 stress 0.1091704 
## Run 12 stress 0.1096264 
## Run 13 stress 0.110784 
## Run 14 stress 0.1135546 
## Run 15 stress 0.1105489 
## Run 16 stress 0.1087888 
## Run 17 stress 0.1091548 
## Run 18 stress 0.1151827 
## Run 19 stress 0.1087028 
## Run 20 stress 0.1099704 
## *** Best solution was not repeated -- monoMDS stopping criteria:
##     20: no. of iterations >= maxit
plot<- plot_ordination(strat_rel.ps, 
                         ps_NMDS,
                         color= "Treatuntreat_EP") +
  ggtitle("NMDS ordination plot - relative abundance") +
  labs(color = "Factor") + 
  theme(axis.text=element_text(size=14), axis.title=element_text(size=14), title =  element_text(size=13), legend.text = element_text(size = 18))

plot_ellipses<-plot +
   stat_ellipse(type = "norm", linetype = 2) +
  theme_bw()

plot_ellipses

#Export NMDS coordinates per sample
write.csv(ps_NMDS[["points"]], file="Results/SOD/RPS10/RPS10_NMDS_coordinates_persample.csv",row.names = TRUE)

Now, we’ll look at ordination plot (rel abundance dataset), with Fire_Mod factor

#Relative abundance
set.seed(1)
ps_NMDS = ordinate(strat_rel.ps, 
                   method="NMDS",
                   distance="bray")
## Run 0 stress 0.109065 
## Run 1 stress 0.1132285 
## Run 2 stress 0.112343 
## Run 3 stress 0.1122217 
## Run 4 stress 0.1086383 
## ... New best solution
## ... Procrustes: rmse 0.08973638  max resid 0.2663288 
## Run 5 stress 0.1090746 
## ... Procrustes: rmse 0.09218401  max resid 0.315221 
## Run 6 stress 0.1097841 
## Run 7 stress 0.1077114 
## ... New best solution
## ... Procrustes: rmse 0.05515573  max resid 0.2686255 
## Run 8 stress 0.1110021 
## Run 9 stress 0.1124331 
## Run 10 stress 0.1085894 
## Run 11 stress 0.1091704 
## Run 12 stress 0.1096264 
## Run 13 stress 0.110784 
## Run 14 stress 0.1135546 
## Run 15 stress 0.1105489 
## Run 16 stress 0.1087888 
## Run 17 stress 0.1091548 
## Run 18 stress 0.1151827 
## Run 19 stress 0.1087028 
## Run 20 stress 0.1099704 
## *** Best solution was not repeated -- monoMDS stopping criteria:
##     20: no. of iterations >= maxit
plot<- plot_ordination(strat_rel.ps, 
                         ps_NMDS,
                         labs(color = "Factor"),
                         color= "Fire_Mod") +
  ggtitle("NMDS ordination plot - relative abundance") +
  theme(axis.text=element_text(size=14), axis.title=element_text(size=14), title =  element_text(size=13), legend.text = element_text(size = 18))
## Warning in plot_ordination(strat_rel.ps, ps_NMDS, labs(color = "Factor"), : type argument not supported. `type` set to 'samples'.
## See `plot_ordination('list')`
plot_ellipses<-plot +
   stat_ellipse(type = "norm", linetype = 2) +
  theme_bw()

plot_ellipses

   stat_ellipse(type = "norm", linetype = 2) +
  theme_bw()

plot_ellipses

Are communities more similar if geographically closeby? Some plots, like wildfire plots are close together, for example

Very preliminary experiment

# Extract OTU table and sample data from phyloseq object
otu_table <- otu_table(strat_rel.ps)
sample_data <- sample_data(strat_rel.ps)

# Transpose OTU table for correct format
otu_table_matrix <- t(as(otu_table, "matrix"))

# Extract longitude and latitude
lon_lat <- cbind(sample_data$Longitude, sample_data$Latitude)

# Calculate distance matrices
geo_dist <- distm(lon_lat, fun = distHaversine)
comm_dist <- vegdist(otu_table_matrix, method = "bray", na.rm = TRUE)

# Convert Bray-Curtis distance to matrix if needed
comm_dist_matrix <- as.matrix(comm_dist)

# Extract sample IDs
sample_ids <- rownames(sample_data)
sample_ids_in_otu <- rownames(otu_table)

# Match sample IDs
common_samples <- intersect(sample_ids_in_otu, sample_ids)
common_indices <- which(sample_ids %in% common_samples)

# Subset distance matrices
geo_dist_aligned <- geo_dist[common_indices, common_indices]
comm_dist_aligned <- comm_dist_matrix[common_indices, common_indices]

# Check dimensions
print(dim(geo_dist_aligned))
## [1] 87 87
print(dim(comm_dist_aligned))
## [1] 87 87
# Perform Mantel test
mantel_result <- mantel(geo_dist_aligned, comm_dist_aligned, method = "spearman", permutations = 999)
print(mantel_result)
## 
## Mantel statistic based on Spearman's rank correlation rho 
## 
## Call:
## mantel(xdis = geo_dist_aligned, ydis = comm_dist_aligned, method = "spearman",      permutations = 999) 
## 
## Mantel statistic r: -0.02314 
##       Significance: 0.853 
## 
## Upper quantiles of permutations (null model):
##    90%    95%  97.5%    99% 
## 0.0296 0.0370 0.0488 0.0573 
## Permutation: free
## Number of permutations: 999
# Extract OTU table and sample data from phyloseq object
otu_table <- otu_table(strat_rel.ps)
sample_data <- sample_data(strat_rel.ps)

# Transpose OTU table for correct format
otu_table_matrix <- as(otu_table, "matrix")

# Calculate Bray-Curtis dissimilarity matrix
comm_dist <- vegdist(otu_table_matrix, method = "bray")

# Run NMDS
nmds_result <- metaMDS(comm_dist, k = 2, trymax = 100)
## Run 0 stress 0.109065 
## Run 1 stress 0.1140198 
## Run 2 stress 0.1092012 
## ... Procrustes: rmse 0.06445074  max resid 0.2734325 
## Run 3 stress 0.1089243 
## ... New best solution
## ... Procrustes: rmse 0.0885924  max resid 0.2516809 
## Run 4 stress 0.1112147 
## Run 5 stress 0.110019 
## Run 6 stress 0.1117029 
## Run 7 stress 0.1078179 
## ... New best solution
## ... Procrustes: rmse 0.0904989  max resid 0.3381792 
## Run 8 stress 0.1078599 
## ... Procrustes: rmse 0.0397632  max resid 0.214336 
## Run 9 stress 0.1104561 
## Run 10 stress 0.1132292 
## Run 11 stress 0.1107878 
## Run 12 stress 0.1108756 
## Run 13 stress 0.10681 
## ... New best solution
## ... Procrustes: rmse 0.05755512  max resid 0.213045 
## Run 14 stress 0.1096509 
## Run 15 stress 0.1110567 
## Run 16 stress 0.1130485 
## Run 17 stress 0.1121968 
## Run 18 stress 0.1090218 
## Run 19 stress 0.111803 
## Run 20 stress 0.1107945 
## Run 21 stress 0.1157787 
## Run 22 stress 0.1105941 
## Run 23 stress 0.1105306 
## Run 24 stress 0.1098904 
## Run 25 stress 0.1119153 
## Run 26 stress 0.1084199 
## Run 27 stress 0.1059131 
## ... New best solution
## ... Procrustes: rmse 0.0342104  max resid 0.2318629 
## Run 28 stress 0.1105374 
## Run 29 stress 0.1122927 
## Run 30 stress 0.1075464 
## Run 31 stress 0.1110512 
## Run 32 stress 0.1086482 
## Run 33 stress 0.110224 
## Run 34 stress 0.1066733 
## Run 35 stress 0.1091809 
## Run 36 stress 0.1119136 
## Run 37 stress 0.1125726 
## Run 38 stress 0.1117885 
## Run 39 stress 0.1123657 
## Run 40 stress 0.1151973 
## Run 41 stress 0.1108687 
## Run 42 stress 0.1096734 
## Run 43 stress 0.1106104 
## Run 44 stress 0.1099517 
## Run 45 stress 0.1127253 
## Run 46 stress 0.1117497 
## Run 47 stress 0.1090427 
## Run 48 stress 0.1115683 
## Run 49 stress 0.1096092 
## Run 50 stress 0.1109108 
## Run 51 stress 0.1103539 
## Run 52 stress 0.1117115 
## Run 53 stress 0.1073624 
## Run 54 stress 0.1095578 
## Run 55 stress 0.1091411 
## Run 56 stress 0.1106969 
## Run 57 stress 0.1134225 
## Run 58 stress 0.1090227 
## Run 59 stress 0.1098357 
## Run 60 stress 0.1089528 
## Run 61 stress 0.111136 
## Run 62 stress 0.1107837 
## Run 63 stress 0.1118999 
## Run 64 stress 0.1124203 
## Run 65 stress 0.1130862 
## Run 66 stress 0.1141762 
## Run 67 stress 0.1068869 
## Run 68 stress 0.1100724 
## Run 69 stress 0.110336 
## Run 70 stress 0.1104363 
## Run 71 stress 0.1160791 
## Run 72 stress 0.1113566 
## Run 73 stress 0.1128454 
## Run 74 stress 0.1088037 
## Run 75 stress 0.1124283 
## Run 76 stress 0.1085502 
## Run 77 stress 0.1090192 
## Run 78 stress 0.107668 
## Run 79 stress 0.1115337 
## Run 80 stress 0.1142718 
## Run 81 stress 0.1115729 
## Run 82 stress 0.1090656 
## Run 83 stress 0.1131809 
## Run 84 stress 0.1086944 
## Run 85 stress 0.1100385 
## Run 86 stress 0.1134094 
## Run 87 stress 0.1107919 
## Run 88 stress 0.1126337 
## Run 89 stress 0.1067496 
## Run 90 stress 0.1106928 
## Run 91 stress 0.1110134 
## Run 92 stress 0.1117553 
## Run 93 stress 0.1110895 
## Run 94 stress 0.1070385 
## Run 95 stress 0.1099693 
## Run 96 stress 0.1108392 
## Run 97 stress 0.1137193 
## Run 98 stress 0.1103742 
## Run 99 stress 0.1085976 
## Run 100 stress 0.1073069 
## *** Best solution was not repeated -- monoMDS stopping criteria:
##     91: no. of iterations >= maxit
##      9: stress ratio > sratmax
# Extract NMDS scores
nmds_scores <- as.data.frame(scores(nmds_result))
nmds_scores$SampleID <- rownames(nmds_scores)

# Extract sample IDs and coordinates correctly
sample_ids <- rownames(sample_data(strat_rel.ps))  # Correct sample IDs
sample_coords <- data.frame(SampleID = sample_ids,
                             Longitude = sample_data(strat_rel.ps)$Longitude,
                             Latitude = sample_data(strat_rel.ps)$Latitude, 
                             Treatuntreat=sample_data(strat_rel.ps)$Treatuntreat_EP,
                             Fire_Mod=sample_data(strat_rel.ps)$Fire_Mod
                            )

# Merge NMDS scores with the correct sample coordinates
nmds_scores <- merge(nmds_scores, sample_coords, by = "SampleID")


# Create NMDS plot with color for Latitude and size for Longitude
nmds_plot <- ggplot(nmds_scores, aes(x = NMDS1, y = NMDS2)) +
  geom_point(aes(color = Latitude, size = Longitude), alpha = 0.7) +  # Use size for Longitude
  labs(title = "NMDS Plot with Latitude and Longitude",
       x = "NMDS Dimension 1",
       y = "NMDS Dimension 2") +
  theme_minimal() +
  scale_color_gradient(low = "blue", high = "red") +  # Color gradient for Latitude
  scale_size_continuous(range = c(2, 6))  # Adjust size range as needed

print(nmds_plot)

nmds_plot <- ggplot(nmds_scores, aes(x = NMDS1, y = NMDS2)) +
  geom_point(aes(color = Latitude, size = Longitude), alpha = 0.7) +
  facet_wrap(~ Fire_Mod) +
  labs(title = "NMDS Plot with Latitude, Longitude, and Treatment",
       x = "NMDS Dimension 1",
       y = "NMDS Dimension 2") +
  theme_minimal() +
  scale_color_gradient(low = "blue", high = "red") +
  scale_size_continuous(range = c(2, 6))

print(nmds_plot)

Functions to prepare to do differential abundance analyses

sample.data.table <- function(ps) {
  as(sample_data(ps), "data.frame") %>%
    as.data.table(keep.rownames = "Sample") %>% 
    setkey(Sample) %>%
    return()
}
asv.data.table <- function(ps) {
  if (taxa_are_rows(ps)) {
    otu.mat <- t(as(otu_table(ps), "matrix"))
  } else {
    otu.mat <- as(otu_table(ps), "matrix")
  }
  return(as.data.table(otu.mat, keep.rownames = "Sample") %>% setkey(Sample))
}
cat.n <- function(x) cat(x, sep = "\n")

Now we import data

sod.data <- sample.data.table(sod_prunereads_5asv.ps)
sod.asvs <- asv.data.table(sod_prunereads_5asv.ps)
sod.asv.ids <- taxa_names(sod_prunereads_5asv.ps)
sod.dt <- sod.data[sod.asvs]

Let’s now do a DESEQ2 analysis-firemod

DESEQ analysis-Fire_mod

set.seed(1)

sod.deseq <- phyloseq_to_deseq2(sod_prunereads_5asv.ps, ~ Fire_Mod)
## converting counts to integer mode
sod.dds <- estimateSizeFactors(sod.deseq, type = 'poscounts')

sod.dds <- DESeq(sod.dds)
## using pre-existing size factors
## estimating dispersions
## gene-wise dispersion estimates
## mean-dispersion relationship
## -- note: fitType='parametric', but the dispersion trend was not well captured by the
##    function: y = a/x + b, and a local regression fit was automatically substituted.
##    specify fitType='local' or 'mean' to avoid this message next time.
## final dispersion estimates
## fitting model and testing
## -- replacing outliers and refitting for 370 genes
## -- DESeq argument 'minReplicatesForReplace' = 7 
## -- original counts are preserved in counts(dds)
## estimating dispersions
## fitting model and testing
all_results <- resultsNames(sod.dds)

all_results <- all_results[all_results != "Intercept"]

process_result <- function(result_name) {
  res.dt <- as.data.table(results(sod.dds, name = result_name), keep.rownames = "ASV")
  
  sig.dt <- res.dt[padj <= 0.05]
  
  sig.dt <- cbind(as(sig.dt, "data.frame"), as(tax_table(sod_prunereads_5asv.ps)[sig.dt$ASV, ], "matrix"))
  
  sig.dt <- sig.dt %>% mutate(across(everything(), ~ifelse(is.na(.), "Unclassified", .)))
  
  write.csv(sig.dt, file = paste0("Results/SOD/RPS10/deseq_", result_name, ".csv"), row.names = TRUE)
  
  plot <- ggplot(sig.dt, aes(y = reorder(ASV, log2FoldChange), x = log2FoldChange, fill = Genus)) +
    geom_vline(xintercept = 0.0, color = "gray", size = 0.5, linetype = "dashed") +
    geom_bar(stat = "identity", position = "dodge", width = 0.8) +
    scale_fill_viridis(discrete = TRUE, option = "D") +
    coord_flip() +
    labs(x = "Log2 Fold Change",
         y = "ASV",
         title = paste("Differential Abundance Analysis:", result_name)) +
    theme_minimal() +
    theme(
      axis.text.y = element_text(size = 8),
      axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1, vjust = 1),
      axis.title = element_text(face = "bold"),
      legend.position = "right",
      legend.title = element_text(face = "bold"),
      plot.title = element_text(size = 16, face = "bold"),
      plot.subtitle = element_text(size = 12),
      panel.grid.major.y = element_blank(),
      panel.grid.minor = element_blank()
    )
  
  ggsave(paste0("Figures/SOD/RPS10/deseq_", result_name, "_barplot.pdf"), plot, width = 20, height = 12)
  
  print(plot)
}
lapply(all_results, process_result)
## Warning in .local(x, row.names, optional, ...): Arguments in '...' ignored
## Warning in .local(x, row.names, optional, ...): Arguments in '...' ignored

## [[1]]

## 
## [[2]]

Let’s now do a DESEQ2 analysis-firemod2

DESEQ analysis-Fire_mod

set.seed(1)

sod_prunereads.ps@sam_data$Fire_Mod <- factor(sod_prunereads.ps@sam_data$Fire_Mod, levels = c( "wildfire", "SODtrt","untreated"))

sod.deseq <- phyloseq_to_deseq2(sod_prunereads_5asv.ps, ~ Fire_Mod)
## converting counts to integer mode
sod.dds <- estimateSizeFactors(sod.deseq, type = 'poscounts')

sod.dds <- DESeq(sod.dds)
## using pre-existing size factors
## estimating dispersions
## gene-wise dispersion estimates
## mean-dispersion relationship
## -- note: fitType='parametric', but the dispersion trend was not well captured by the
##    function: y = a/x + b, and a local regression fit was automatically substituted.
##    specify fitType='local' or 'mean' to avoid this message next time.
## final dispersion estimates
## fitting model and testing
## -- replacing outliers and refitting for 370 genes
## -- DESeq argument 'minReplicatesForReplace' = 7 
## -- original counts are preserved in counts(dds)
## estimating dispersions
## fitting model and testing
all_results <- resultsNames(sod.dds)

all_results <- all_results[all_results != "Intercept"]

process_result <- function(result_name) {
  res.dt <- as.data.table(results(sod.dds, name = result_name), keep.rownames = "ASV")
  
  sig.dt <- res.dt[padj <= 0.05]
  
  sig.dt <- cbind(as(sig.dt, "data.frame"), as(tax_table(sod_prunereads_5asv.ps)[sig.dt$ASV, ], "matrix"))
  
  sig.dt <- sig.dt %>% mutate(across(everything(), ~ifelse(is.na(.), "Unclassified", .)))
  
  write.csv(sig.dt, file = paste0("Results/SOD/RPS10/deseq_", result_name, ".csv"), row.names = TRUE)
  
  plot <- ggplot(sig.dt, aes(y = reorder(ASV, log2FoldChange), x = log2FoldChange, fill = Genus)) +
    geom_vline(xintercept = 0.0, color = "gray", size = 0.5, linetype = "dashed") +
    geom_bar(stat = "identity", position = "dodge", width = 0.8) +
    scale_fill_viridis(discrete = TRUE, option = "D") +
    coord_flip() +
    labs(x = "Log2 Fold Change",
         y = "ASV",
         title = paste("Differential Abundance Analysis:", result_name)) +
    theme_minimal() +
    theme(
      axis.text.y = element_text(size = 12),
      axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, size=12),
      axis.title = element_text(face = "bold"),
      legend.position = "right",
      legend.title = element_text(face = "bold"),
      plot.title = element_text(size = 16, face = "bold"),
      plot.subtitle = element_text(size = 12),
      panel.grid.major.y = element_blank(),
      panel.grid.minor = element_blank()
    )
  
  ggsave(paste0("Figures/SOD/RPS10/deseq_", result_name, "_barplot.pdf"), plot, width = 20, height = 12)
  
  print(plot)
}
lapply(all_results, process_result)
## Warning in .local(x, row.names, optional, ...): Arguments in '...' ignored
## Warning in .local(x, row.names, optional, ...): Arguments in '...' ignored

## [[1]]

## 
## [[2]]

### DESEQ analysis-treatuntreat

set.seed(1)

sod.deseq <- phyloseq_to_deseq2(sod_prunereads_5asv.ps, ~ Treatuntreat_EP)
## converting counts to integer mode
sod.dds <- estimateSizeFactors(sod.deseq, type = 'poscounts')

sod.dds <- DESeq(sod.dds)
## using pre-existing size factors
## estimating dispersions
## gene-wise dispersion estimates
## mean-dispersion relationship
## -- note: fitType='parametric', but the dispersion trend was not well captured by the
##    function: y = a/x + b, and a local regression fit was automatically substituted.
##    specify fitType='local' or 'mean' to avoid this message next time.
## final dispersion estimates
## fitting model and testing
## -- replacing outliers and refitting for 382 genes
## -- DESeq argument 'minReplicatesForReplace' = 7 
## -- original counts are preserved in counts(dds)
## estimating dispersions
## fitting model and testing
all_results <- resultsNames(sod.dds)

all_results <- all_results[all_results != "Intercept"]

process_result <- function(result_name) {
  res.dt <- as.data.table(results(sod.dds, name = result_name), keep.rownames = "ASV")
  
  sig.dt <- res.dt[padj <= 0.05]
  
  sig.dt <- cbind(as(sig.dt, "data.frame"), as(tax_table(sod_prunereads_5asv.ps)[sig.dt$ASV, ], "matrix"))
  
  sig.dt <- sig.dt %>% mutate(across(everything(), ~ifelse(is.na(.), "Unclassified", .)))
  
  write.csv(sig.dt, file = paste0("Results/SOD/RPS10/deseq_", result_name, ".csv"), row.names = TRUE)
  
  plot <- ggplot(sig.dt, aes(y = reorder(ASV, log2FoldChange), x = log2FoldChange, fill = Genus)) +
    geom_vline(xintercept = 0.0, color = "gray", size = 0.5, linetype = "dashed") +
    geom_bar(stat = "identity", position = "dodge", width = 0.8) +
    scale_fill_viridis(discrete = TRUE, option = "D") +
    coord_flip() +
    labs(x = "Log2 Fold Change",
         y = "ASV",
         title = paste("Differential Abundance Analysis:", result_name)) +
    theme_minimal() +
    theme(
      axis.text.y = element_text(size = 12),
      axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1, vjust = 1, size=12),
      axis.title = element_text(face = "bold"),
      legend.position = "right",
      legend.title = element_text(face = "bold"),
      plot.title = element_text(size = 16, face = "bold"),
      plot.subtitle = element_text(size = 12),
      panel.grid.major.y = element_blank(),
      panel.grid.minor = element_blank()
    )
  
  ggsave(paste0("Figures/SOD/RPS10/deseq_", result_name, "_barplot.pdf"), plot, width = 20, height = 12)
  
  print(plot)
}
lapply(all_results, process_result)
## Warning in .local(x, row.names, optional, ...): Arguments in '...' ignored

## [[1]]

DESEQ analysis-yearsincetrt

set.seed(1)

sod.deseq <- phyloseq_to_deseq2(sod_prunereads_5asv.ps, ~ YrSinceTrt_BR)
## converting counts to integer mode
sod.dds <- estimateSizeFactors(sod.deseq, type = 'poscounts')

sod.dds <- DESeq(sod.dds)
## using pre-existing size factors
## estimating dispersions
## gene-wise dispersion estimates
## mean-dispersion relationship
## -- note: fitType='parametric', but the dispersion trend was not well captured by the
##    function: y = a/x + b, and a local regression fit was automatically substituted.
##    specify fitType='local' or 'mean' to avoid this message next time.
## final dispersion estimates
## fitting model and testing
## -- replacing outliers and refitting for 352 genes
## -- DESeq argument 'minReplicatesForReplace' = 7 
## -- original counts are preserved in counts(dds)
## estimating dispersions
## fitting model and testing
all_results <- resultsNames(sod.dds)

all_results <- all_results[all_results != "Intercept"]

process_result <- function(result_name) {
  res.dt <- as.data.table(results(sod.dds, name = result_name), keep.rownames = "ASV")
  
  sig.dt <- res.dt[padj <= 0.05]
  
  sig.dt <- cbind(as(sig.dt, "data.frame"), as(tax_table(sod_prunereads_5asv.ps)[sig.dt$ASV, ], "matrix"))
  
  sig.dt <- sig.dt %>% mutate(across(everything(), ~ifelse(is.na(.), "Unclassified", .)))
  
  write.csv(sig.dt, file = paste0("Results/SOD/RPS10/deseq_", result_name, ".csv"), row.names = TRUE)
  
  plot <- ggplot(sig.dt, aes(y = reorder(ASV, log2FoldChange), x = log2FoldChange, fill = Genus)) +
    geom_vline(xintercept = 0.0, color = "gray", size = 0.5, linetype = "dashed") +
    geom_bar(stat = "identity", position = "dodge", width = 0.8) +
    scale_fill_viridis(discrete = TRUE, option = "D") +
    coord_flip() +
    labs(x = "Log2 Fold Change",
         y = "ASV",
         title = paste("Differential Abundance Analysis:", result_name)) +
    theme_minimal() +
    theme(
      axis.text.y = element_text(size = 12),
      axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1, vjust = 1, size=12),
      axis.title = element_text(face = "bold"),
      legend.position = "right",
      legend.title = element_text(face = "bold"),
      plot.title = element_text(size = 16, face = "bold"),
      plot.subtitle = element_text(size = 12),
      panel.grid.major.y = element_blank(),
      panel.grid.minor = element_blank()
    )
  
  ggsave(paste0("Figures/SOD/RPS10/deseq_", result_name, "_barplot.pdf"), plot, width = 20, height = 12)
  
  print(plot)
}
lapply(all_results, process_result)
## Warning in .local(x, row.names, optional, ...): Arguments in '...' ignored
## Warning in .local(x, row.names, optional, ...): Arguments in '...' ignored

## Warning in .local(x, row.names, optional, ...): Arguments in '...' ignored

## Warning in .local(x, row.names, optional, ...): Arguments in '...' ignored

## [[1]]

## 
## [[2]]

## 
## [[3]]

## 
## [[4]]

For nicer visuals, let’s make heat trees (specifically the comparison between untreated and treated samples), and we’ll start with Wilcoxon rank sum-treatment

Convert taxmap to phyloseq obj

remove_boot_columns <- function(x) {
  x <- x[, !grepl("boot", colnames(x))]
  return(x)
}

sod_5asv_tax <- tax_table(sod_prunereads_5asv.ps) %>%
  as.data.frame() %>%
  remove_boot_columns() %>%
  as.matrix() %>%
  tax_table()

tax_table(sod_prunereads_5asv.ps) <- sod_5asv_tax

obj_RPS10<-parse_phyloseq(sod_prunereads_5asv.ps)
## The following 331 of 415 (79.8%) input indexes have `NA` in their classifications:
##    2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 7, 8, 9 ... 409, 410, 411, 412, 413, 414, 415
obj_RPS10_rarefy <-parse_phyloseq(rarefied.ps)
## The following 307 of 389 (78.9%) input indexes have `NA` in their classifications:
##    2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 7, 8, 9 ... 383, 384, 385, 386, 387, 388, 389
obj_RPS10$data$tax_abund <- calc_taxon_abund(obj_RPS10, data = "otu_table", cols = obj_RPS10$data$sample_data$sample_id)
## Summing per-taxon counts from 87 columns for 35 taxa
obj_RPS10_rarefy$data$tax_abund <- calc_taxon_abund(obj_RPS10_rarefy , data = "otu_table", cols = obj_RPS10_rarefy$data$sample_data$sample_id)
## Summing per-taxon counts from 87 columns for 1126 taxa
obj_RPS10$data$abund_prop <- calc_obs_props(obj_RPS10, data = "otu_table", cols = obj_RPS10$data$sample_data$sample_id)
## Calculating proportions from counts for 87 columns for 415 observations.
obj_RPS10$data$tax_prop <- calc_taxon_abund(obj_RPS10, data = "abund_prop", cols = obj_RPS10$data$sample_data$sample_id)
## Summing per-taxon counts from 87 columns for 35 taxa
obj_RPS10$data$tax_prop$all_samples <- rowMeans(obj_RPS10$data$tax_prop[,obj_RPS10$data$sample_data$sample_id])

We’ll then do DESEQ analysis on untreated vs. untreated samples

set.seed(1)
obj_RPS10$data$diff_table_deseq <- metacoder::calc_diff_abund_deseq2(obj_RPS10,
                                      data = "tax_abund",
                                      cols = obj_RPS10$data$sample_data$sample_id, # What columns of sample data to
                                      groups = obj_RPS10$data$sample_data$Treatuntreat_EP) # What category each sample is assigned to
## converting counts to integer mode
## Warning in DESeqDataSet(se, design = design, ignoreRank): some variables in
## design formula are characters, converting to factors
## estimating size factors
## estimating dispersions
## gene-wise dispersion estimates
## mean-dispersion relationship
## -- note: fitType='parametric', but the dispersion trend was not well captured by the
##    function: y = a/x + b, and a local regression fit was automatically substituted.
##    specify fitType='local' or 'mean' to avoid this message next time.
## final dispersion estimates
## fitting model and testing
## -- replacing outliers and refitting for 25 genes
## -- DESeq argument 'minReplicatesForReplace' = 7 
## -- original counts are preserved in counts(dds)
## estimating dispersions
## fitting model and testing
obj_RPS10$data$diff_table_deseq$log2FoldChange[obj_RPS10$data$diff_table_deseq$padj > 0.05] <- 0

range(obj_RPS10$obj_RPS10$data$diff_table_deseq$padj, finite = TRUE) 
## Warning in min(x, na.rm = na.rm): no non-missing arguments to min; returning
## Inf
## Warning in max(x, na.rm = na.rm): no non-missing arguments to max; returning
## -Inf
## [1]  Inf -Inf
range(obj_RPS10$data$diff_table_deseq$log2FoldChange, finite = TRUE) 
## [1] -29.39350  28.45218
obj_RPS10<- metacoder::filter_taxa(obj_RPS10, taxon_names != "None", reassign_obs = FALSE)
obj_RPS10 <- metacoder::filter_taxa(obj_RPS10, taxon_names != "", reassign_obs = FALSE)
obj_RPS10 <- metacoder::filter_taxa(obj_RPS10, taxon_names != "NA", reassign_obs = FALSE)


obj_RPS10 %>%
  metacoder::filter_taxa(taxon_ranks == "Species", supertaxa = TRUE, reassign_obs = FALSE) %>%
  heat_tree_matrix(data = "diff_table_deseq",
                   node_label = taxon_names,
                   node_size = n_obs, # number of OTUs
                   node_color = log2FoldChange, # difference between groups
                   node_color_trans = "linear",
                   node_color_interval = c(-1, 1)*max(abs(range(obj_RPS10$data$diff_table_deseq$log2FoldChange))), 
                   edge_color_interval = c(-1, 1)*max(abs(range(obj_RPS10$data$diff_table_deseq$log2FoldChange))),  
                   node_color_range = diverging_palette(), # diverging colors
                   node_size_axis_label = "ASV count",
                   node_color_axis_label = "Log 2 Fold Change",
                   layout = "da", initial_layout = "re",
                   key_size = 0.4,
                   output_file = "Figures/SOD/RPS10/diff_abundance_heattree_deseq_sod_RPS10_trtuntreat.pdf") # The layout algorithm that initializes node locations

Make tree-deseq-Fire_Mod

set.seed(1)
obj_RPS10$data$diff_table_deseq <- metacoder::calc_diff_abund_deseq2(obj_RPS10,
                                      data = "tax_abund",
                                      cols = obj_RPS10$data$sample_data$sample_id, # What columns of sample data to
                                      groups = obj_RPS10$data$sample_data$Fire_EP) # What category each sample is assigned to
## converting counts to integer mode
## Warning in DESeqDataSet(se, design = design, ignoreRank): some variables in
## design formula are characters, converting to factors
## estimating size factors
## estimating dispersions
## gene-wise dispersion estimates
## mean-dispersion relationship
## -- note: fitType='parametric', but the dispersion trend was not well captured by the
##    function: y = a/x + b, and a local regression fit was automatically substituted.
##    specify fitType='local' or 'mean' to avoid this message next time.
## final dispersion estimates
## fitting model and testing
## -- replacing outliers and refitting for 22 genes
## -- DESeq argument 'minReplicatesForReplace' = 7 
## -- original counts are preserved in counts(dds)
## estimating dispersions
## fitting model and testing
obj_RPS10$data$diff_table_deseq$log2FoldChange[obj_RPS10$data$diff_table_deseq$padj > 0.05] <- 0

range(obj_RPS10$obj_RPS10$data$diff_table_deseq$padj, finite = TRUE) 
## Warning in min(x, na.rm = na.rm): no non-missing arguments to min; returning
## Inf
## Warning in max(x, na.rm = na.rm): no non-missing arguments to max; returning
## -Inf
## [1]  Inf -Inf
range(obj_RPS10$data$diff_table_deseq$log2FoldChange, finite = TRUE) 
## [1] -29.96726  37.48047
obj_RPS10<- metacoder::filter_taxa(obj_RPS10, taxon_names != "None", reassign_obs = FALSE)
obj_RPS10 <- metacoder::filter_taxa(obj_RPS10, taxon_names != "", reassign_obs = FALSE)
obj_RPS10 <- metacoder::filter_taxa(obj_RPS10, taxon_names != "NA", reassign_obs = FALSE)


obj_RPS10 %>%
  metacoder::filter_taxa(taxon_ranks == "Species", supertaxa = TRUE, reassign_obs = FALSE) %>%
  heat_tree_matrix(data = "diff_table_deseq",
                   node_label = taxon_names,
                   node_size = n_obs, # number of OTUs
                   node_color = log2FoldChange, # difference between groups
                   node_color_trans = "linear",
                   node_color_interval = c(-28, 28), # symmetric interval
                   edge_color_interval = c(-28, 28), # symmetric interval
                   node_color_range = diverging_palette(), # diverging colors
                   node_size_axis_label = "ASV count",
                   node_color_axis_label = "Log 2 Fold Change",
                   layout = "da", initial_layout = "re",
                   key_size = 0.4,
                   output_file = "Figures/SOD/RPS10/diff_abundance_heattree_deseq_sod_RPS10_firemod.pdf") # The layout algorithm that initializes node locations

We’ll then do DESEQ analysis on YearSinceTrt_BR

set.seed(1)
obj_RPS10$data$diff_table_deseq <- metacoder::calc_diff_abund_deseq2(obj_RPS10,
                                      data = "tax_abund",
                                      cols = obj_RPS10$data$sample_data$sample_id, # What columns of sample data to
                                      groups = obj_RPS10$data$sample_data$YrSinceTrt_BR) # What category each sample is assigned to
## converting counts to integer mode
## Warning in DESeqDataSet(se, design = design, ignoreRank): some variables in
## design formula are characters, converting to factors
## estimating size factors
## estimating dispersions
## gene-wise dispersion estimates
## mean-dispersion relationship
## -- note: fitType='parametric', but the dispersion trend was not well captured by the
##    function: y = a/x + b, and a local regression fit was automatically substituted.
##    specify fitType='local' or 'mean' to avoid this message next time.
## final dispersion estimates
## fitting model and testing
## -- replacing outliers and refitting for 19 genes
## -- DESeq argument 'minReplicatesForReplace' = 7 
## -- original counts are preserved in counts(dds)
## estimating dispersions
## fitting model and testing
obj_RPS10$data$diff_table_deseq$log2FoldChange[obj_RPS10$data$diff_table_deseq$padj > 0.05] <- 0

range(obj_RPS10$obj_RPS10$data$diff_table_deseq$padj, finite = TRUE) 
## Warning in min(x, na.rm = na.rm): no non-missing arguments to min; returning
## Inf
## Warning in max(x, na.rm = na.rm): no non-missing arguments to max; returning
## -Inf
## [1]  Inf -Inf
range(obj_RPS10$data$diff_table_deseq$log2FoldChange, finite = TRUE) 
## [1] -50.34088  49.77277
obj_RPS10<- metacoder::filter_taxa(obj_RPS10, taxon_names != "None", reassign_obs = FALSE)
obj_RPS10 <- metacoder::filter_taxa(obj_RPS10, taxon_names != "", reassign_obs = FALSE)
obj_RPS10 <- metacoder::filter_taxa(obj_RPS10, taxon_names != "NA", reassign_obs = FALSE)


obj_RPS10 %>%
  metacoder::filter_taxa(taxon_ranks == "Species", supertaxa = TRUE, reassign_obs = FALSE) %>%
  heat_tree_matrix(data = "diff_table_deseq",
                   node_label = taxon_names,
                   node_size = n_obs, # number of OTUs
                   node_color = log2FoldChange, # difference between groups
                   node_color_trans = "linear",
                   node_color_interval = c(-38, 38), # symmetric interval
                   edge_color_interval = c(-38, 38), # symmetric interval
                   node_color_range = diverging_palette(), # diverging colors
                   node_size_axis_label = "ASV count",
                   node_color_axis_label = "Log 2 Fold Change",
                   layout = "da", initial_layout = "re",
                   key_size = 0.4,
                   output_file = "Figures/SOD/RPS10/diff_abundance_heattree_deseq_sod_RPS10_YrsincetrtBr.pdf") # The layout algorithm that initializes node locations

##  matrixStats          * 1.3.0     2024-04-11 [1] CRAN (R 4.1.2)
##  memoise                2.0.1     2021-11-26 [1] CRAN (R 4.1.2)
##  metacoder            * 0.3.7     2024-02-20 [1] CRAN (R 4.1.2)
##  mgcv                   1.8-39    2022-02-24 [4] CRAN (R 4.1.2)
##  multtest               2.50.0    2021-10-26 [1] Bioconductor
